commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer product direction are still required before automated repair; the code defects are concrete but should be fixed after that direction is settled.; Needs attention: The diff still has a concrete security-boundary concern because the advertised offline mode can inherit cached GitHub CLI auth.; Review finding: [P1] Isolate gh config and prompt for local-range — src/clawsweeper.ts:... (sha=3eae0ed3eb25a482c5934d6941c4510600a7c344)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `--local-range`, `--allow-closed`, `--body-file`, and `--additional-policy` for full local review, extracts shared local-review helpers, and adds local-range tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection against PR head gives a high-confidence path: compare the new `localRange` branch with current `localReviewCommand` and inspect the generated `pullFiles`, Codex auth flags, and synthetic author metadata. I did not execute the PR command because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer product direction are still required before automated repair; the code defects are concrete but should be fixed after that direction is settled.

target.title: feat(review): offline `--local-range` mode for pre-submission review (#357 follow-on)
target.body:
## Summary

#357 added `review --local-only` — the **full** proof-aware review (`review-item.md` +
decision schema, real-behavior-proof + Mantis assessments) for advisory review before
submitting a PR. But it still requires the item to be **open on GitHub**: it fetches the
item, diff, body, and comments via the API (`--item-number`) and rejects closed items. So
you can't review your work *before* opening the PR — which is the stated goal.

This adds `--local-range`: run that **same full review** on the local git range
(`merge-base(--base, HEAD)..HEAD`) with **no GitHub fetch**. `Item` + `ItemContext` are
synthesized from `git` (diff from `git diff`, body from the commit message or `--body-file`),
so the full review — including the real-behavior-proof and Mantis decision — runs offline,
before a PR exists, and on fork checkouts that the gh-fetch `gitInfo` path rejects.

### Relationship to #298 (not a duplicate — its full-review counterpart)

#298 added offline committed-range review on **Commit Sweeper** — the **code-only** lane.
This is the **full proof-aware** counterpart: the same offline committed-range idea, but on
the #357 full-review path (proof + Mantis), which #298's code-only review does not cover. It
deliberately **reuses #298's offline guardrails** rather than paralleling them:

- the **clean-checkout contract** (`dirtyWorktree` — rejects a dirty tree; the committed-range
  review can't see staged/untracked work);
- **GitHub-credential scrubbing** (`scrubGitHubCredentialEnv`) + **web search disabled**
  (`LOCAL_REVIEW_WEB_SEARCH_CONFIG`) — the same offline guarantee (no GitHub reads, no network);
- `commitMetadata(offline=true)` for author/subject/dates.

`dirtyWorktree` and `scrubGitHubCredentialEnv` are **extracted** from #298's
`localReviewCommand` (behavior there unchanged) and shared between the two offline paths.

### Composing affordances

- `--allow-closed`: review closed/merged items (regression fixtures, hypothetical re-review).
- `--body-file <file>`: substitute the PR body in the prompt — to test the proof / Mantis
  decision against a hypothetical body, or supply the body when reviewing a local range.
- `--additional-policy <file>`: layer a repo-specific policy file onto the review prompt.

## Changes

- `--local-range` (with `--base <ref>`, default `origin/main`): `buildLocalRangeReview()`
  builds a synthetic `Item` + `ItemContext` (`pullFiles` from `git diff`, body from the commit
  message) and `reviewCommand` branches the offline `gitInfo` (`{ mainSha, latestRelease: null }`,
  no fetch), the synthetic candidate, and the synthetic context, and suppresses the start comment.
- **#298 reuse**: clean-checkout guard (`dirtyWorktree`), `scrubGitHubCredentialEnv`,
  `web_search="disabled"` on the offline engine, and `commitMetadata(offline=true)` — the
  first two extracted into shared helpers in `commit-sweeper.ts`.
- `--allow-closed`: relaxes the open-only selection gate in `selectCandidates`.
- `--body-file`: injects an authoritative PR-body section into the review prompt.
- `--additional-policy`: reads a policy file and layers it onto the review prompt.

## Validation

- `pnpm run check` (build, lint, unit + repair tests, changed + full coverage, format):
  passes — `fail 0`, format clean. #298's `local-review` tests unaffected by the shared-helper
  extraction.
- `test/local-range-review.test.ts` (6 cases): the synthesized item/context/diff, the
  title + `origin/main`-base fallbacks, the empty-diff case, the **dirty-tree refusal**, and
  the empty-range throw.
- Offline review of a real fork checkout produced a valid Decision with **no GitHub access** —
  the diff was read from `git diff`, not the API:

  ```
  decision: keep_open · confidence: high
  realBehaviorProof.status:    missing       (the commit body carried no after-fix proof)
  telegramVisibleProof.status: not_needed
  mantisRecommendation.status: not_recommended
  ```

## Scope / notes

- Advisory/offline only: `--local-range` implies no GitHub writes (no start comment, no posted
  review) and no GitHub reads (credentials scrubbed, web search off).
- `--local-range` reviews **committed** work — it requires a clean checkout (commit or stash first).
- Engine selection is intentionally out of scope for this PR.
